response.justification: The candidate is a capable generalist full-stack/web developer with broad experience (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) and some historical C/C++ exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI, a C++ bachelor thesis). However, this role is embedded LAN-switch security firmware requiring strong C for UNIX development, Linux kernel investigation and source modification, and switch-level security/networking knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3) — none of which appear in the resume. Critically, native-level Japanese is a hard requirement, and the candidate lists only English (fluent), Romanian (fluent), and basic German. The combination of missing language requirement and absent core domain experience makes this a poor fit.
strengths: Deep, long software-engineering track record well beyond the 3+ year minimum
Some real C/C++ experience (LabWindows/CVI IDE components, C++ thesis) plus Linux familiarity
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, Docker, dev-ops, and embedded/robotics exposure
concerns: No native-level Japanese — a stated hard requirement
No demonstrated Linux kernel source modification, switch/networking (L2/L3), or security tech (MACsec, 802.1X) experience
Recent work is web/full-stack in TypeScript/Node; C is not a current core skill
